Threat, Invariant, Divergence, and Independent-Review Register The threat and invariant registries SHALL enumerate every material adversary class and mandatory safety property required by the final product logic; their record counts are informational rather than normative. Each invariant records planned enforcement functions, current enforcement evidence where available, consensus state keys, tests, evidence artifacts, and verification status. Mainnet cannot activate while a P0 threat lacks implemented controls and independent adversarial evidence. T-001-Malicious or compromised user account: signature/nonce enforcement; PoH; rate limits; public receipts; recovery and revocation T-002-Malicious validator minority: HotStuff quorum; public evidence; non-economic suspension; validator-set epoch transitions T-003-Malicious validator supermajority: independent control policy; social recovery/fork governance; external monitoring; constitutional incident procedure T-004-Malicious juror or reviewer: randomized independent assignment; conflict exclusions; commit-reveal or sealed submission; appeals; reputation T-005-Compromised emissary: proposal-bound spend; multisig; staged milestones; independent evidence review; restitution T-006-Corrupt storage provider: CID binding; capacity probes; challenge/revalidation; holdback; reputation and suspension T-007-Compromised seed/trust registry: repository-pinned threshold-signed registries; multiple trust roots; peer diversity; operator verification T-008-Network partition or eclipse: BFT safety halt; round/view changes; peer diversity; no wall-clock finality; partition recovery transcript T-009-Recovery-credential compromise or continuity fraud: offline recovery-key separation, immediate old-key/device/session revocation, independent Tier 2 continuity panel, per-account rate limits, public receipts, and post-recovery cooldown T-010-Public-data surveillance or retaliation: data minimization; no public raw PoH evidence; sealed retention; client warnings; export controls for sensitive indexes T-011-Ordinary signing-key compromise: device/session scope, rapid revocation, offline recovery-key or Tier 2 continuity recovery, anomaly notices, hardware-backed keys, and post-recovery restrictions T-012-Mempool/API/peer denial of service: bounded queues; per-account limits; fair scheduling; backpressure; peer bans; degraded-mode observability T-013-State-bloat attack: object size limits; rate limits; storage accounting; pruning commitments; state-growth SLO T-014-Malicious compiler/build/dependency: pinned toolchain; two-party reproducible builds; CycloneDX SBOM; SLSA provenance; threshold signatures T-015-Snapshot poisoning/fast-sync attack: snapshot commitments in finalized chain; validator certificate; independent replay samples; wrong-root rejection T-016-Long-range history or archival withholding: checkpoint trust roots; multiple archives; full replay option; history availability monitoring T-017-Reward farming/economic Sybil: PoH; group activity rules; common-control detection; caps; public allocation receipts T-018-Group proliferation/common-control evasion: verified human membership; overlap analysis; common-control disclosure; allocation caps T-019-Coercion, vote buying, retaliation, or targeted censorship: public participation without public choice attribution; sealed receipt-resistant ballots; independent proposal access; anti-retaliation disputes; sanctions; metadata-risk disclosure; external cryptographic review T-020-Metadata correlation: minimal metadata; exact retention; no private-content claims; client-side privacy notices; aggregation limits T-021-Client-side transaction substitution: canonical human-readable signing preview; domain separation; payload hash confirmation; wallet route allowlist T-022-Malicious migration vector: signed vector hashes; pre/post roots; multi-node rehearsal; forward-only compensating repair T-023-Biased randomness/reviewer sampling: chain-committed seed; deterministic sampling; exclusion proofs; statistical audit T-024-Operator collusion below identity limits: independence attestations; infrastructure diversity; common funding disclosure; external validator audit T-025-Emergency-governance abuse: severity panels; higher thresholds; bounded duration; tranche caps; automatic expiry; post-action review 88.1 Independent review controls Review Status Evidence boundary Cryptographic: entropy, ballots, PoH evidence, publisher signature PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No named independent reviewer or report exists. Consensus and validator epoch handoff PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW Internal specification algorithm is defined; no independent implementation audit exists. Privacy and metadata correlation PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No external privacy review or ballot transport analysis exists. Accessibility and usability PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW Technical PDF checks may pass; no external screen-reader or low-vision study exists. Legal and compliance PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No jurisdiction-specific legal opinion exists. Supply chain and reproducible build PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No two independent Candidate 3.11 implementation builds or review report exists. 89. Canonical Executable Specification Vector Register 69 contract-bound reference vectors are defined. Each bundle contains active-profile identifiers, a complete canonical Section 85 state object, an actual Section 86 contract envelope and payload, delivery or restart schedule, exact typed RCP bytes, a Section 87 failure ID when rejected, expected post-state bytes, roots, vector scope, and bundle digest. The test-only weall-spec-fixture-auth-v1 witness is deterministic and has no production authority. Profile-gated vectors do not claim cryptographic PASS evidence. No repository runner or independent implementation PASS is claimed. rows.reason_or_contract: GENESIS_RECOVERY_CONFLICT bootstrap_or_consensus: Two distinct valid recovery certificates exist for the same sequence. SYS:C310:0039 Stage: peer_admission_or_sys_due Nonce: not_applicable Receipt: required only after accepted MSG aggregation or due SYS execution; pre-admission rejection emits none. State: unchanged except explicit FAILED_TERMINAL recovery conflict. Retry: corrected certificate or governed recovery only Lifecycle: remains halted or becomes FAILED_TERMINAL as specified. HTTP 409 conflict, 422 invalid evidence, or peer-protocol rejection with {failure_id,code,stage,retryable}. 88. Threat, Invariant, Divergence, and Independent-Review Register The threat and invariant registries SHALL enumerate every material adversary class and mandatory safety property required by the final product logic; their record counts are informational rather than normative. Each invariant records planned enforcement functions, current enforcement evidence where available, consensus state keys, tests, evidence artifacts, and verification status. Mainnet cannot activate while a P0 threat lacks implemented controls and independent adversarial evidence. T-001-Malicious or compromised user account: signature/nonce enforcement; PoH; rate limits; public receipts; recovery and revocation T-002-Malicious validator minority: HotStuff quorum; public evidence; non-economic suspension; validator-set epoch transitions T-003-Malicious validator supermajority: independent control policy; social recovery/fork governance; external monitoring; constitutional incident procedure T-004-Malicious juror or reviewer: randomized independent assignment; conflict exclusions; commit-reveal or sealed submission; appeals; reputation T-005-Compromised emissary: proposal-bound spend; multisig; staged milestones; independent evidence review; restitution T-006-Corrupt storage provider: CID binding; capacity probes; challenge/revalidation; holdback; reputation and suspension T-007-Compromised seed/trust registry: repository-pinned threshold-signed registries; multiple trust roots; peer diversity; operator verification T-008-Network partition or eclipse: BFT safety halt; round/view changes; peer diversity; no wall-clock finality; partition recovery transcript T-009-Recovery-credential compromise or continuity fraud: offline recovery-key separation, immediate old-key/device/session revocation, independent Tier 2 continuity panel, per-account rate limits, public receipts, and post-recovery cooldown T-010-Public-data surveillance or retaliation: data minimization; no public raw PoH evidence; sealed retention; client warnings; export controls for sensitive indexes T-011-Ordinary signing-key compromise: device/session scope, rapid revocation, offline recovery-key or Tier 2 continuity recovery, anomaly notices, hardware-backed keys, and post-recovery restrictions T-012-Mempool/API/peer denial of service: bounded queues; per-account limits; fair scheduling; backpressure; peer bans; degraded-mode observability T-013-State-bloat attack: object size limits; rate limits; storage accounting; pruning commitments; state-growth SLO T-014-Malicious compiler/build/dependency: pinned toolchain; two-party reproducible builds; CycloneDX SBOM; SLSA provenance; threshold signatures T-015-Snapshot poisoning/fast-sync attack: snapshot commitments in finalized chain; validator certificate; independent replay samples; wrong-root rejection T-016-Long-range history or archival withholding: checkpoint trust roots; multiple archives; full replay option; history availability monitoring T-017-Reward farming/economic Sybil: PoH; group activity rules; common-control detection; caps; public allocation receipts T-018-Group proliferation/common-control evasion: verified human membership; overlap analysis; common-control disclosure; allocation caps T-019-Coercion, vote buying, retaliation, or targeted censorship: public participation without public choice attribution; sealed receipt-resistant ballots; independent proposal access; anti-retaliation disputes; sanctions; metadata-risk disclosure; external cryptographic review T-020-Metadata correlation: minimal metadata; exact retention; no private-content claims; client-side privacy notices; aggregation limits T-021-Client-side transaction substitution: canonical human-readable signing preview; domain separation; payload hash confirmation; wallet route allowlist T-022-Malicious migration vector: signed vector hashes; pre/post roots; multi-node rehearsal; forward-only compensating repair T-023-Biased randomness/reviewer sampling: chain-committed seed; deterministic sampling; exclusion proofs; statistical audit T-024-Operator collusion below identity limits: independence attestations; infrastructure diversity; common funding disclosure; external validator audit T-025-Emergency-governance abuse: severity panels; higher thresholds; bounded duration; tranche caps; automatic expiry; post-action review 88.1 Independent review controls Review Status Evidence boundary Cryptographic: entropy, ballots, PoH evidence, publisher signature PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No named independent reviewer or report exists. Consensus and validator epoch handoff PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW Internal specification algorithm is defined; no independent implementation audit exists. Privacy and metadata correlation PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No external privacy review or ballot transport analysis exists. Accessibility and usability PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW Technical PDF checks may pass; no external screen-reader or low-vision study exists. Legal and compliance PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No jurisdiction-specific legal opinion exists. Supply chain and reproducible build PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No two independent Candidate 3.11 implementation builds or review report exists. 89. Canonical Executable Specification Vector Register 69 contract-bound reference vectors are defined. Each bundle contains active-profile identifiers, a complete canonical Section 85 state object, an actual Section 86 contract envelope and payload, delivery or restart schedule, exact typed RCP bytes, a Section 87 failure ID when rejected, expected post-state bytes, roots, vector scope, and bundle digest. The test-only weall-spec-fixture-auth-v1 witness is deterministic and has no production authority. Profile-gated vectors do not claim cryptographic PASS evidence. No repository runner or independent implementation PASS is claimed. HTTP 409 conflict, 422 invalid evidence, or peer-protocol rejection with {failure_id,code,stage,retryable}. 88. Threat, Invariant, Divergence, and Independent-Review Register The threat and invariant registries SHALL enumerate every material adversary class and mandatory safety property required by the final product logic; their record counts are informational rather than normative. Each invariant records planned enforcement functions, current enforcement evidence where available, consensus state keys, tests, evidence artifacts, and verification status. Mainnet cannot activate while a P0 threat lacks implemented controls and independent adversarial evidence. T-001-Malicious or compromised user account: signature/nonce enforcement; PoH; rate limits; public receipts; recovery and revocation T-002-Malicious validator minority: HotStuff quorum; public evidence; non-economic suspension; validator-set epoch transitions T-003-Malicious validator supermajority: independent control policy; social recovery/fork governance; external monitoring; constitutional incident procedure T-004-Malicious juror or reviewer: randomized independent assignment; conflict exclusions; commit-reveal or sealed submission; appeals; reputation T-005-Compromised emissary: proposal-bound spend; multisig; staged milestones; independent evidence review; restitution T-006-Corrupt storage provider: CID binding; capacity probes; challenge/revalidation; holdback; reputation and suspension T-007-Compromised seed/trust registry: repository-pinned threshold-signed registries; multiple trust roots; peer diversity; operator verification T-008-Network partition or eclipse: BFT safety halt; round/view changes; peer diversity; no wall-clock finality; partition recovery transcript T-009-Recovery-credential compromise or continuity fraud: offline recovery-key separation, immediate old-key/device/session revocation, independent Tier 2 continuity panel, per-account rate limits, public receipts, and post-recovery cooldown T-010-Public-data surveillance or retaliation: data minimization; no public raw PoH evidence; sealed retention; client warnings; export controls for sensitive indexes T-011-Ordinary signing-key compromise: device/session scope, rapid revocation, offline recovery-key or Tier 2 continuity recovery, anomaly notices, hardware-backed keys, and post-recovery restrictions T-012-Mempool/API/peer denial of service: bounded queues; per-account limits; fair scheduling; backpressure; peer bans; degraded-mode observability T-013-State-bloat attack: object size limits; rate limits; storage accounting; pruning commitments; state-growth SLO T-014-Malicious compiler/build/dependency: pinned toolchain; two-party reproducible builds; CycloneDX SBOM; SLSA provenance; threshold signatures T-015-Snapshot poisoning/fast-sync attack: snapshot commitments in finalized chain; validator certificate; independent replay samples; wrong-root rejection T-016-Long-range history or archival withholding: checkpoint trust roots; multiple archives; full replay option; history availability monitoring T-017-Reward farming/economic Sybil: PoH; group activity rules; common-control detection; caps; public allocation receipts T-018-Group proliferation/common-control evasion: verified human membership; overlap analysis; common-control disclosure; allocation caps T-019-Coercion, vote buying, retaliation, or targeted censorship: public participation without public choice attribution; sealed receipt-resistant ballots; independent proposal access; anti-retaliation disputes; sanctions; metadata-risk disclosure; external cryptographic review T-020-Metadata correlation: minimal metadata; exact retention; no private-content claims; client-side privacy notices; aggregation limits T-021-Client-side transaction substitution: canonical human-readable signing preview; domain separation; payload hash confirmation; wallet route allowlist T-022-Malicious migration vector: signed vector hashes; pre/post roots; multi-node rehearsal; forward-only compensating repair T-023-Biased randomness/reviewer sampling: chain-committed seed; deterministic sampling; exclusion proofs; statistical audit T-024-Operator collusion below identity limits: independence attestations; infrastructure diversity; common funding disclosure; external validator audit T-025-Emergency-governance abuse: severity panels; higher thresholds; bounded duration; tranche caps; automatic expiry; post-action review 88.1 Independent review controls Review Status Evidence boundary Cryptographic: entropy, ballots, PoH evidence, publisher signature PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No named independent reviewer or report exists. Consensus and validator epoch handoff PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW Internal specification algorithm is defined; no independent implementation audit exists. Privacy and metadata correlation PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No external privacy review or ballot transport analysis exists. Accessibility and usability PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW Technical PDF checks may pass; no external screen-reader or low-vision study exists. Legal and compliance PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No jurisdiction-specific legal opinion exists. Supply chain and reproducible build PENDING_EXTERNAL_REVIEW No two independent Candidate 3.11 implementation builds or review report exists. 89. Each bundle contains active-profile identifiers, a complete canonical Section 85 state object, an actual Section 86 contract envelope and payload, delivery or restart schedule, exact typed RCP bytes, a Section 87 failure ID when rejected, expected post-state bytes, roots, vector scope, and bundle digest. The test-only weall-spec-fixture-auth-v1 witness is deterministic and has no production authority. Profile-gated vectors do not claim cryptographic PASS evidence. No repository runner or independent implementation PASS is claimed.
